item.title: US Defense Secretary Hegseth Joins Trump on Rare Beijing Trip
item.description: Pete Hegseth becomes the first Pentagon chief to accompany a US president to Beijing since Nixon's 1972 visit, signaling the strategic importance of the Trump-Xi summit amid ongoing Iran tensions.

item.title: China Warns US on Arms Sales to Taiwan Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Beijing reiterated strong opposition to US weapons sales to Taiwan just hours before Trump arrives, calling on Washington to honor its commitments and threatening to crush any independence bid.

item.title: Sirens Warn of Suspected Hezbollah Drone Attack in Northern Israel
item.description: Air raid sirens sounded in northern Israel amid a suspected Hezbollah drone intrusion from Lebanon, raising fears of a new front opening as the Iran conflict continues.

item.title: Ukrainian Drones Strike Port Infrastructure in Russia's Krasnodar Krai
item.description: Ukrainian forces targeted port facilities near the Kerch Peninsula east of Crimea, continuing their campaign against Russian logistics in the occupied territories.

item.title: UK Activists Face Terrorist Sentencing for Raid on Israeli Arms Firm
item.description: Four Palestine Action members convicted of smashing a UK site of Israeli defense firm Elbit could be sentenced as terrorists, marking a significant escalation in legal consequences for pro-Palestine activism.

item.title: Trump says Ukraine war very close to ending as Kremlin echoes claim
item.description: President Donald Trump said the war in Ukraine is "very close" to ending, while the Kremlin repeated Putin's assertion that the conflict is nearly over. Ukrainian President Zelenskiy has expressed skepticism about Moscow's intentions.

item.title: Putin announces deployment of new Sarmat nuclear missile this year
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Russia will deploy its new Sarmat strategic nuclear missile, described as "the most powerful in the world," by the end of this year. The announcement follows a successful test launch of the ICBM known as Satan II.

item.title: US and China agree no country should exact Hormuz shipping tolls
item.description: Senior US and Chinese officials agreed that no country should be allowed to impose shipping tolls in the Strait of Hormuz, signaling cooperation to pressure Iran over control of the vital waterway.

item.title: US EIA warns Middle East oil disruptions worse than previously estimated
item.description: The US Energy Information Administration revised forecasts to reflect a much larger and longer hit to global oil supplies from the Iran war than previously projected, with the Strait of Hormuz expected to remain shut through late May.

item.title: US Treasury Secretary Bessent arrives in South Korea for pre-summit China trade talks
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent arrived in South Korea on Wednesday for trade talks with Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng ahead of the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing, as the world's two largest economies seek to navigate trade tensions and the Iran conflict.

item.title: Taiwan president thanks US for defense support ahead of Beijing summit
item.description: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te expressed gratitude to the US for helping strengthen the island's defenses and said Taipei would not give in to pressure, ahead of the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing.

item.title: Zelensky faces major corruption test as ex-chief of staff named suspect in graft probe
item.description: Ukrainian anti-corruption agencies have named President Zelensky's former chief of staff as an official suspect in a money-laundering investigation, marking a significant escalation of graft allegations reaching close to the presidency.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz emerges as central battleground of Iran conflict
item.description: Reuters analysis: The passage of a handful of oil and gas tankers in recent days with Tehran's consent hints at tacit acceptance of Iranian control, foreshadowing a more dangerous phase in what is fast becoming a Hormuz war.

item.title: Iran sets five conditions for resuming talks with US
item.description: Iran demands ending the war on all fronts, lifting sanctions, releasing frozen assets, compensating for war damage, and recognizing sovereignty rights over the Strait of Hormuz before any new negotiations with Washington.

item.title: Ukraine sanctions 66 Russian entities over military supply chain
item.description: President Zelensky imposed sanctions on 32 Russian companies and 34 individuals involved in supplying materials for ballistic missiles, surface-to-air systems, and drone production.

item.title: EU expresses frustration over slow defense industry ramp-up
item.description: EU diplomat Kaja Kallas said the bloc is frustrated that despite billions in funding, defense production lags behind Ukraine, which has become a significant military-industrial power in four years.

item.title: Major fire breaks out at Moscow tourist complex
item.description: A massive fire exceeding 3,000 square meters engulfed the Izmailovo Kremlin cultural and entertainment complex in eastern Moscow overnight, with the cause unclear.

item.title: UK affirms Ukraine support amid domestic political turmoil
item.description: UK Europe Minister Stephen Doughty assured continued British backing for Ukraine despite Labour Party losses in local elections and pressure on PM Starmer.

item.title: Trump Golden Dome missile shield to cost $1.2 trillion
item.description: US Congressional Budget Office estimates Trump's proposed missile defense system will cost nearly seven times his initial estimate. The system may not be able to stop an all-out missile attack.

item.title: Global conflict displacements hit record 32.3 million in 2025
item.description: Internal displacements caused by violence or conflict reached a record high last year, surpassing disaster-driven displacement for the first time. Total global displacement now stands at 82.2 million.

item.title: Israeli strikes in Lebanon kill 13 including two paramedics
item.description: Lebanon's health ministry says paramedics were carrying out a rescue mission in Nabatieh when they were killed. The strikes came in response to earlier attacks.

item.title: US Inflation Surges to 3.8% as Iran War Drives Energy Prices Higher
item.description: Consumer prices spiked in April with petrol prices up nearly 30% since last year, driven by the Iran conflict's impact on global oil markets and the Strait of Hormuz blockage.

item.title: Uganda's Museveni Sworn In for Record Seventh Term After Disputed Election
item.description: President Yoweri Museveni, 81, won January's disputed elections to extend his 40-year rule, making him one of Africa's longest-serving leaders.

item.title: Serbia Hosts First Joint Military Exercise With NATO Since Kosovo War
item.description: The two-week drills mark a historic shift for Serbia, coming less than 30 years after NATO bombed Belgrade during the Kosovo conflict.

item.title: Dali Ship Operator Charged Criminally for Baltimore Bridge Collapse
item.description: Federal prosecutors filed criminal charges against Synergy Marine for the Key Bridge collapse that killed six workers. The company faces conspiracy, obstruction, and misconduct charges.
